purpose: Each household recites aloud the names of debts owed and grievances held before breaking fast, ensuring nothing festers unspoken
When entering another's home, visitors expose their most prominent scar as proof they have suffered and thus can be trusted
One hour after sundown, all speech ceases; business, arguments, and confessions must wait until the hour passes
Doorways are lined with ash each morning, swept away each night—a visual record that the home was undisturbed
The final morsel of every meal is left untouched on the plate, acknowledging that nothing is truly finished
Citizens introduce themselves with the name of their most recent dead relative appended to their own, keeping the deceased in public memory
Monthly, citizens cut a lock of hair and surrender it to communal pyres, externalizing private sorrow into shared smoke
When concluding any negotiation, both parties draw a drop of their own blood onto the contract as a seal of accountability
Households call out their number of living members at nightfall; neighbors listen and note discrepancies
Before sleep, all pockets and bags must be turned inside-out and shown empty, symbolizing one carries no hidden burdens into dreams
